description:
You are acting as a front-end web developer who has inherited a small project located under /home/user/project.  
Inside the project there is already a folder /home/user/project/assets/css/ containing several “*.css” style-sheets.  
Management has asked you to prepare a **repeatable, script-friendly report** that shows every 6-digit hexadecimal colour used anywhere in those style-sheets, together with the number of times each colour appears.

Perform the following, step by step, **only with normal user privileges**:

1. Scan every file whose name matches the glob /home/user/project/assets/css/style_*.css.  
   • A “6-digit colour” is a hash sign followed by exactly six hexadecimal digits, i.e. the regex `#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}`.  
   • Treat colour codes in a case-insensitive manner, so “#ff0000” and “#FF0000” are the same colour.  
   • Ignore any three-digit short-hand colours (e.g. #FFF) or RGBA/HSL functions.

2. Count how many times each distinct 6-digit colour occurs across the entire set of matched files.

3. Create the directory /home/user/project/reports/ if it does not already exist.

4. Write the results to a new text file called  
   /home/user/project/reports/color_palette.txt  
   following **exactly** this format so that automated tests can verify it:  
   • One colour per line.  
   • Lines must be sorted in ascending ASCII order by the upper-case, hash-less colour code (e.g. “0000FF” comes before “00FF00”).  
   • Each line must look like  
     `<UPPERCASE_HEX> : <INTEGER_COUNT>`  
     For example:  
     `FF0000 : 3`  
   • There must be a single trailing newline character at the end of the file and **no extra blank lines** anywhere else.

5. For traceability, also create the directory /home/user/project/logs/ (if necessary) and append an entry to the file  
   /home/user/project/logs/process.log  
   in the following fixed format **for each CSS file that your script touches**:  
   `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - processed /full/path/to/file`  
   • Use the current local time for each timestamp.  
   • Append (do not overwrite) so repeated runs keep growing the log.  
   • Make sure every log line ends with a newline.

You may use any combination of standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities (grep, sed, awk, sort, uniq, etc.) or a short shell script—whatever you prefer—as long as the final two files exist and follow the exact formats described above.

When you are finished, you should have:

• /home/user/project/reports/color_palette.txt — containing the deduplicated, counted and sorted list of colours.  
• /home/user/project/logs/process.log — containing at least one correctly formatted line for each processed CSS file.

The automated grader will inspect those two files and nothing else, so be meticulous about their exact names, locations, and formats.
truth:
Initial file system state provided to the agent before the task starts:

Directories:
/home/user/project/
/home/user/project/assets/
/home/user/project/assets/css/

Files and their contents (Unix LF endings):

1. /home/user/project/assets/css/style_main.css
------------------------------------------------
body { background-color: #ff0000; color: #00ff00; }
h1   { color: #ff0000; }
p    { border: 1px solid #0000ff; }
------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/project/assets/css/style_theme.css
------------------------------------------------
.nav    { background: #ABC123; }
.footer { background: #ff0000; }
.btn    { background: #abc123; }
.header { color: #FFF; }   /* 3-digit code must be ignored */
------------------------------------------------

No other files exist inside /home/user/project/ at start-up.

Expected final state after correct task completion:

1. The directory /home/user/project/reports/ exists (permission 755, owned by the normal user).

2. The file /home/user/project/reports/color_palette.txt exists (permission 644, owned by the normal user) and its exact byte-for-byte contents are:

0000FF : 1
00FF00 : 1
ABC123 : 2
FF0000 : 3

(There is a single trailing newline after the last “3”. No blank line after that.)

3. The directory /home/user/project/logs/ exists (permission 755).

4. The file /home/user/project/logs/process.log exists (permission 644) and contains **at least** the following two lines (additional lines are allowed if the agent re-ran its script):

YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - processed /home/user/project/assets/css/style_main.css
YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - processed /home/user/project/assets/css/style_theme.css

where “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS” are valid timestamps representing when the agent actually processed each file. Lines must be terminated by '\n'.

These three verification points (existence & precise contents of color_palette.txt, existence & pattern of lines in process.log) are what the automated tests will enforce.
